title: arum orientale subsp. danicum
intro: Arum orientale subsp. danicum, also known as the Egyptian arum or Danicum arum lily, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the Arum genus, characterized by its large, glossy leaves and trumpet-shaped flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its ability to act as a diuretic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant, which can help to relieve respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. The health benefits of Arum orientale subsp. danicum include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate joint pain and swelling, and support the immune system. The plant is typically prepared as a herbal tea or infusion, and can also be used in tincture or powdered form for its medicinal properties.
